text:
A tree hydrated but some attributes of the server rendered HTML didn't match the client properties. This won't be patched up. This can happen if a SSR-ed Client Component used:

- A server/client branch `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`.
- Variable input such as `Date.now()` or `Math.random()` which changes each time it's called.
- Date formatting in a user's locale which doesn't match the server.
- External changing data without sending a snapshot of it along with the HTML.
- Invalid HTML tag nesting.

It can also happen if the client has a browser extension installed which messes with the HTML before React loaded.

%s%s https://react.dev/link/hydration-mismatch 

  ...
    <ScrollAndMaybeFocusHandler cacheNode={{rsc:{...}, ...}}>
      <InnerScrollAndFocusHandlerOld focusAndScrollRef={{scrollRef:null, ...}} cacheNode={{rsc:{...}, ...}}>
        <ErrorBoundary errorComponent={undefined} errorStyles={undefined} errorScripts={undefined}>
          <LoadingBoundary name="degree-lev..." loading={null}>
            <HTTPAccessFallbackBoundary notFound={undefined} forbidden={undefined} unauthorized={undefined}>
              <RedirectBoundary>
                <RedirectErrorBoundary router={{...}}>
                  <InnerLayoutRouter url="/admin/deg..." tree={[...]} params={{}} cacheNode={{rsc:{...}, ...}} ...>
                    <SegmentViewNode type="page" pagePath="admin/degr...">
                      <SegmentTrieNode>
                      <DegreeLevelsPage>
                        <SimpleEntityManager title="Degree Levels" singularLabel="Degree Level" items={[...]} ...>
                          <div className="space-y-6">
                            <div className="flex items...">
                              <div>
                              <div className="flex items...">
                                <input
                                  value=""
                                  onChange={function onChange}
                                  placeholder="Search"
                                  className="rounded-lg border border-gray-300 px-3 py-2 text-sm outline-none focus:bo..."
-                                 style={{caret-color:"transparent"}}
                                >
                                ...
                            ...
                    ...
                  ...
        ...
Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 409 (Conflict)
Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 400 (Bad Request)

A tree hydrated but some attributes of the server rendered HTML didn't match the client properties. This won't be patched up. This can happen if a SSR-ed Client Component used:

- A server/client branch `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`.
- Variable input such as `Date.now()` or `Math.random()` which changes each time it's called.
- Date formatting in a user's locale which doesn't match the server.
- External changing data without sending a snapshot of it along with the HTML.
- Invalid HTML tag nesting.

It can also happen if the client has a browser extension installed which messes with the HTML before React loaded.

%s%s https://react.dev/link/hydration-mismatch
